name: Browned-Butter Protein Cookies (Soft-Chewy)
description: VERSION B - Even & Soft-Chewy: soft and chewy edge to edge, the same texture throughout with no raw-dough center. A pure-procedure variant of the gooey base - identical dough, ingredients, and macros; only the bake (steps 4-6) differs. Flattening the cookie and lowering the oven temp both shrink the edge-vs-center cook gap, so the whole cookie finishes together. Spreads a little more, no molten center.

payload.text: The brown sugar (28 g real) provides the molasses chew and spread; the 3 g Splenda tops off sweetness without adding moisture, so the ratio is fixed — don't swap one for the other.

payload.instructionText: Chill only ~15 minutes (or let cold dough sit 10 min at room temp before baking). A fridge-cold center lags the most, so less chilling helps the middle keep pace.
Shape into 3 cookies and flatten into discs about 1/2 inch (1.5 cm) thick.
Bake at 325F / 160C on the middle rack for ~11-13 minutes. Pull when set but the centers still feel slightly soft.

payload.text: Let the browned butter cool 2–3 min before adding eggs — pour it in hot and you'll scramble the egg and break the emulsion, giving greasy cookies with a curdled look.
325F for 11–13 min and pull while centers feel slightly soft — 40 g whey in a small batch overcooks in minutes, turning the center chalky. Carryover on the hot tray finishes them.
Stir in the dry ingredients until the dough just comes together; overmixing develops the AP flour's gluten and toughens the protein cookies into dense pucks.

Don't move or taste the cookies until they've rested 10–15 min on the tray — protein cookies feel floppy straight from the oven and firm up entirely as they cool; judging early will make you think they're underbaked.
